2604.06753.title: Select-then-Solve: Paradigm Routing as Inference-Time Optimization for LLM Agents
2604.06753.paper_summary: When an LLM-based agent improves on a task, is the gain from the model itself or from the reasoning paradigm wrapped around it? We study this question by comparing six inference-time paradigms, namely Direct, CoT, ReAct, Plan-Execute, Reflection, and ReCode, across four frontier LLMs and ten benchmarks, yielding roughly 18,000 runs. We find that reasoning structure helps dramatically on some tasks but hurts on others: ReAct improves over Direct by 44pp on GAIA, while CoT degrades performance by 15pp on HumanEval. No single paradigm dominates, and oracle per-task selection beats the best fixed paradigm by 17.1pp on average. Motivated by this complementarity, we propose a select-then-solve approach: before answering each task, a lightweight embedding-based router selects the most suitable paradigm. Across four models, the router improves average accuracy from 47.6% to 53.1%, outperforming the best fixed paradigm at 50.3% by 2.8pp and recovering up to 37% of the oracle gap. In contrast, zero-shot self-routing only works for GPT-5 at 67.1% and fails for weaker models, all trailing the learned router. Our results argue that reasoning paradigm selection should be a per-task decision made by a learned router, not a fixed architectural choice.
2604.06753.paper_summary_zh: 當基於 LLM 的代理在某項任務上有所改善時，這一增益是來自模型本身還是包裹在其周圍的推理範式？我們通過比較六種推理時間範式，即直接推理（Direct）、鏈式推理（CoT）、反應（ReAct）、計劃執行（Plan-Execute）、反思（Reflection）和重編碼（ReCode），在四個前沿 LLM 和十個基準上進行研究，總計約 18,000 次運行。我們發現推理結構在某些任務上有顯著幫助，但在其他任務上則有負面影響：在 GAIA 上，ReAct 比直接推理提高了 44 個百分點，而 CoT 則在 HumanEval 上降低了 15 個百分點。沒有單一的範式主導，並且每個任務的最佳選擇超過了最佳固定範式，平均提高了 17.1 個百分點。受到這種互補性的啟發，我們提出了一種選擇後解決的方法：在回答每個任務之前，一個輕量級的嵌入式路由器選擇最合適的範式。在四個模型中，該路由器將平均準確率從 47.6% 提高到 53.1%，超過最佳固定範式 50.3% 的 2.8 個百分點，並且恢復了高達 37% 的預測差距。相比之下，零樣本自我路由僅對 GPT-5 有效，準確率為 67.1%，對較弱的模型則失敗，所有這些模型的表現都不及學習的路由器。我們的結果表明，推理範式的選擇應該是由學習的路由器根據每個任務做出的決定，而不是固定的架構選擇。
2604.06753.author: Heng Zhou et.al.
2604.06753.authors: Heng Zhou, Zelin Tan, Zhemeng Zhang, Yutao Fan, Yibing Lin, Li Kang, Xiufeng Song, Rui Li, Songtao Huang, Ao Yu, Yuchen Fan, Yanxu Chen, Kaixin Xu, Xiaohong Liu, Yiran Qin, Philip Torr, Chen Zhang, Zhenfei Yin
